task: How many times was a Twitter/X post cited as a reference on the english Wikipedia pages for each day of August in the last June 2023 versions of the pages?

trajectory.action:
file_saver({"content": "Title: Scope and Counting Rules — Twitter/X Citations on English Wikipedia August Day Pages (Last June 2023 Versions)

Version: Step 0 (Scope and Rules Definition)
Date: 2025-12-21

1) Scope
- Pages: English Wikipedia date-pages for every day of August: "August 1" through "August 31" (31 pages total). Namespace: Main (article) namespace.
- Language/Project: enwiki (English Wikipedia).
- Target revision for each page: The last revision within June 2023 (UTC). Formally, select the most recent revision whose timestamp is between 2023-06-01T00:00:00Z and 2023-06-30T23:59:59Z, inclusive. If multiple, choose the one with the latest timestamp within this window.
- Fallback if no June 2023 revision exists for a page: Select the latest revision strictly before 2023-07-01T00:00:00Z (i.e., <= 2023-06-30T23:59:59Z). If still none (e.g., page created after July 1, 2023), record as "no eligible revision" and assign count = 0, with a flag in metadata.

2) What to Count
- Objective: For each target page revision, count the number of unique references that cite a specific Twitter/X post (a "tweet").
- Unit of counting: Unique reference entries (i.e., distinct <ref>...</ref> items as they would appear in the References list), not the number of times a named reference is reused in-text.
- Definition of a "Twitter/X post (tweet) citation": A reference whose source ultimately points to a specific tweet. This includes any of the following:
  a) URLs to twitter.com or x.com with a tweet permalink pattern, e.g.,
     - https://twitter.com/{user}/status/{numeric_id}
     - https://x.com/{user}/status/{numeric_id}
     - Historic variants: /statuses/{numeric_id}
     - Mobile/subdomain variants: https://m.twitter.com/{user}/status/{id}, https://mobile.twitter.com/{user}/status/{id}
  b) References using the {{Cite tweet}} template (or equivalent tweet-specific citation templates) with parameters indicating a tweet (e.g., |user=, |author=, |id=, |url= pointing to a tweet). Count as a tweet citation even if URL is missing, provided the template denotes a specific tweet via parameters (e.g., |id= present).
  c) Archived snapshots of tweet URLs via archival services (e.g., web.archive.org, archive.is, archive.today, archive.ph). If the archived URL’s original target (embedded in the archive path) resolves to a tweet permalink as described in (a), count it.

3) Inclusions and Exclusions
- Include:
  - twitter.com and x.com top-level domains and any subdomains (e.g., m.twitter.com, mobile.twitter.com, www.x.com if applicable), provided the path pattern indicates a tweet permalink as above.
  - Shortened URLs (e.g., t.co, bit.ly, tinyurl.com, is.gd, ow.ly, goo.gl, etc.) that ultimately resolve to a tweet permalink on twitter.com or x.com after HTTP redirection.
  - {{Cite tweet}} and equivalent tweet-citation templates even without explicit URLs, if tweet-identifying parameters are present.
  - Archived links to tweets from recognized archival domains when the underlying original is a tweet permalink.
- Exclude:
  - Links to Twitter/X profiles, timelines, search pages, or site home pages that are not specific tweet permalinks.
  - Links solely to Twitter-hosted media endpoints (e.g., pbs.twimg.com, video.twimg.com) without a tweet permalink.
  - Third-party front-ends or mirrors of Twitter content (e.g., nitter.net) unless the reference also clearly specifies the original tweet permalink on twitter.com or x.com.
  - Non-reference external links (e.g., External links section not within <ref> tags) — only <ref>-based citations are counted.

4) Normalization and URL Resolution
- URL normalization steps (applied for identification and deduplication):
  - Lowercase scheme and host; preserve path/case sensitivity as applicable.
  - Remove tracking/query parameters that do not affect tweet identity (e.g., utm_*). For tweets, the identity is determined by domain + canonical path up to the numeric tweet ID.
  - Standardize common mobile/WWW subdomain variants to canonical host for comparison (e.g., m.twitter.com -> twitter.com; mobile.twitter.com -> twitter.com; www.x.com -> x.com).
  - For archived URLs, extract the original URL segment and apply normalization to the original.
- Redirect/shortener resolution:
  - Follow HTTP redirects for known shorteners (t.co, bit.ly, tinyurl.com, is.gd, ow.ly, goo.gl, etc.) up to a reasonable limit.
  - If resolution fails (timeout, HTTP error), fallback logic:
    * If the reference uses {{Cite tweet}} with tweet-identifying params, count it as a tweet citation.
    * Else if the un-resolved short URL domain is t.co and the surrounding citation text/template explicitly indicates a tweet (e.g., publisher=Twitter, work=Twitter, template=Cite tweet), count it.
    * Otherwise, do not count it as a tweet citation.

5) Deduplication Rules
- Primary: Deduplicate by reference identity. Two references are considered the same tweet citation if, after normalization:
  - They resolve to the same tweet permalink (same domain canonicalization + same numeric tweet ID path), OR
  - They are separate <ref> entries that both use {{Cite tweet}} and indicate the same user and same numeric tweet ID.
- Named references reused in-text: Count once (only the defining <ref name="..."> with content forms the unique reference; any <ref name="..."/> reuses are not additional counts).
- Unnamed <ref> entries that independently point to the same tweet permalink count as one after normalization and deduplication.

6) Parsing Sources
- We will parse both:
  - Wikitext of the selected revision to extract <ref> blocks and templates (e.g., {{Cite tweet}}), and
  - The parsed HTML for cross-verification of rendered citations and to catch edge cases from transclusions.
- Priority is given to wikitext extraction for precise template detection; HTML parsing is used for reconciliation and QC.

7) Quality Control
- Cross-check counts between wikitext-based and HTML-based extractions; investigate and resolve mismatches.
- Manually spot-check a sample of pages and citations to confirm tweet determination and deduplication behavior.
- Log ambiguous cases (e.g., unresolved shorteners without clear tweet indication) and apply the fallback rules consistently.

8) Output per Page
- Metadata to record: page title, selected revision ID, revision timestamp, whether a June-2023-in-month revision was found, and the final tweet-citation count.
- Also record intermediate diagnostics: number of total references, number of candidate Twitter/X references before and after URL resolution, number deduplicated, and notes on any unresolved/ambiguous cases.

9) Final Deliverables
- Per-day counts for August 1–31 (ordered list), where each value is the number of unique Twitter/X post citations on that page’s last June 2023 revision.
- A concise comma-separated list of 31 integers matching Aug 1 through Aug 31, prepared for the FINAL ANSWER.
- A methods and assumptions appendix summarizing the above rules.

10) Assumptions
- "Last June 2023 version" is interpreted as the most recent revision in June 2023 (UTC). If absent, the nearest earlier revision before July 2023 is used as a fallback; otherwise, the page is marked with zero and flagged.
- Only tweet permalinks (or Cite tweet template with tweet ID) constitute "Twitter/X post" citations; general Twitter pages or media asset hosts do not.
- Deduplication targets unique cited tweets, not the number of in-text citation uses.

This document defines the scope, counting criteria, normalization, deduplication, and QC approach for subsequent steps.", "file_path": "work_space/gaia_50f58759_20251222_063700_771398/scope_and_counting_rules_step0.md", "mode": "w"})

trajectory.action:
execute_code({"code": "import os, json, requests, csv, time
from datetime import datetime

WS = 'work_space/gaia_50f58759_20251222_063700_771398'
DATA_DIR = os.path.join(WS, 'data', 'revisions_august')
os.makedirs(DATA_DIR, exist_ok=True)

S = requests.Session()
S.headers.update({'User-Agent': 'GAIA-Tool/1.0 (data-retrieval for analysis) email:not-provided'})
API = 'https://en.wikipedia.org/w/api.php'

IN_START = '2023-06-01T00:00:00Z'
IN_END = '2023-06-30T23:59:59Z'
FALLBACK_START = '2023-07-01T00:00:00Z'

results_summary = []

for day in range(1, 32):
    title = f"August {day}"
    # First: try to get last revision within June 2023 (latest in window)
    params = {
        'action': 'query',
        'prop': 'revisions',
        'titles': title,
        'rvslots': 'main',
        'rvprop': 'ids|timestamp|content',
        'rvlimit': 1,
        'rvstart': IN_END,
        'rvend': IN_START,
        'rvdir': 'older',
        'format': 'json'
    }
    try:
        r = S.get(API, params=params, timeout=30)
        r.raise_for_status()
        data = r.json()
    except Exception as e:
        data = {'error': str(e)}
    page = None
    rev = None
    in_month = False

    if 'query' in data and 'pages' in data['query']:
        for _, p in data['query']['pages'].items():
            page = p
            break
        if page and 'revisions' in page:
            rev = page['revisions'][0]
            in_month = True
    
    # Fallback: latest before July 1, 2023
    if rev is None:
        params_fb = {
            'action': 'query',
            'prop': 'revisions',
            'titles': title,
            'rvslots': 'main',
            'rvprop': 'ids|timestamp|content',
            'rvlimit': 1,
            'rvstart': FALLBACK_START,
            'rvdir': 'older',
            'format': 'json'
        }
        try:
            r2 = S.get(API, params=params_fb, timeout=30)
            r2.raise_for_status()
            data2 = r2.json()
        except Exception as e:
            data2 = {'error': str(e)}
        if 'query' in data2 and 'pages' in data2['query']:
            for _, p in data2['query']['pages'].items():
                page = p
                break
            if page and 'revisions' in page:
                rev = page['revisions'][0]
                in_month = False

    record = {
        'title': title,
        'pageid': page.get('pageid') if isinstance(page, dict) else None,
        'in_month_revision': in_month,
        'rev_id': None,
        'rev_timestamp': None,
        'wikitext': None,
        'html': None,
        'errors': []
    }

    if rev:
        rev_id = rev.get('revid') or rev.get('rev_id')
        ts = rev.get('timestamp')
        # Extract wikitext content across possible keys
        content = None
        try:
            slots = rev.get('slots') or {}
            main = slots.get('main') or {}
            content = main.get('*') if '*' in main else main.get('content')
        except Exception:
            content = None
        if content is None:
            # Legacy content key
            content = rev.get('*') or rev.get('content')
        record['rev_id'] = rev_id
        record['rev_timestamp'] = ts
        record['wikitext'] = content

        # Fetch parsed HTML using parse API
        if rev_id is not None:
            try:
                r3 = S.get(API, params={'action': 'parse', 'oldid': rev_id, 'prop': 'text', 'format': 'json'}, timeout=30)
                r3.raise_for_status()
                d3 = r3.json()
                html = d3.get('parse', {}).get('text', {}).get('*')
                record['html'] = html
            except Exception as e:
                record['errors'].append(f'parse_html_error: {e}')
    else:
        record['errors'].append('no_eligible_revision')

    # Save per-day JSON
    out_path = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, f'august_{day:02d}.json')
    with open(out_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        json.dump(record, f, ensure_ascii=False)

    # Append to summary
    results_summary.append({
        'day': day,
        'title': title,
        'pageid': record['pageid'],
        'in_month_revision': record['in_month_revision'],
        'rev_id': record['rev_id'],
        'rev_timestamp': record['rev_timestamp'],
        'wikitext_len': len(record['wikitext']) if record['wikitext'] else 0,
        'html_len': len(record['html']) if record['html'] else 0,
        'errors': ';'.join(record['errors']) if record['errors'] else ''
    })
    time.sleep(0.2)

# Save manifest CSV
manifest_path = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'manifest.csv')
with open(manifest_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8', newline='') as f:
    writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=list(results_summary[0].keys()))
    writer.writeheader()
    for row in results_summary:
        writer.writerow(row)

print({'saved_dir': DATA_DIR, 'files_saved': len(os.listdir(DATA_DIR))})"})

{{Day}}

==Events==
===Pre-1600===
*[[30 BC]] – [[Augustus|Octavian]] (later known as Augustus) enters [[Alexandria]], [[Egypt]], bringing it under the control of the [[Roman Republic]].
*[[AD 69]] – [[Revolt of the Batavi|Batavian rebellion]]: The [[Batavi (Germanic tribe)|Batavians]] in [[Germania Inferior]] (Netherlands) revolt under the leadership of [[Gaius Julius Civilis]].
* [[527]] – [[Justinian I]] becomes the sole ruler of the [[Byzantine Empire]].
* [[607]] – [[Ono no Imoko]] is dispatched as envoy to the [[Sui dynasty|Sui]] court in China (Traditional [[Japanese calendar|Japanese date]]: July 3, 607).
* [[902]] – [[Taormina]], the last Byzantine stronghold in [[Sicily]], is [[Siege of Taormina (902)|captured]] by the [[Aghlabid]] army, concluding the [[Muslim conquest of Sicily]].<ref>{{Byzance et les Arabes|volume=2.1|pages=145–147}}</ref>
*[[1203]] – [[Isaac II Angelos]], restored [[Byzantine Emperor]], declares his son [[Alexios IV Angelos]] co-emperor after pressure from the forces of the [[Fourth Crusade]].
*[[1291]] – The [[Old Swiss Confederacy]] is formed with the signature of the [[Federal Charter of 1291|Federal Charter]].
*[[1469]] – [[Louis XI of France]] founds the [[chivalric order]] called the [[Order of Saint Michael]] in [[Amboise]].
*[[1498]] – [[Christopher Columbus]] becomes the first European to visit what is now [[Venezuela]].
*[[1571]] – The [[Ottoman–Venetian War (1570–73)#Ottoman conquest of Cyprus|Ottoman conquest of Cyprus]] is concluded, by the surrender of [[Famagusta]].
===1601–1900===
*[[1620]] – [[Speedwell (1577 ship)|''Speedwell'']] leaves [[Delfshaven]] to bring [[Pilgrim Fathers|pilgrims]] to [[Plymouth Colony|America]] by way of England.
*[[1664]] – [[Ottoman Empire|Ottoman forces]] are defeated in the [[Battle of Saint Gotthard (1664)|battle of Saint Gotthard]] by an [[Habsburg monarchy|Austrian]] army led by [[Raimondo Montecuccoli]], resulting in the [[Peace of Vasvár]].
*[[1714]] – George, Elector of [[Hanover]], becomes King [[George I of Great Britain]], marking the beginning of the [[Georgian era]] of British history.
*[[1759]] – [[Seven Years' War]]: The [[Battle of Minden]], an allied Anglo-German army victory over the French. In Britain this was one of a number of events that constituted the [[Annus Mirabilis of 1759]] and is celebrated as [[Minden Day]] by certain British Army regiments.
*[[1774]] – British scientist [[Joseph Priestley]] discovers [[oxygen]] gas, corroborating the prior discovery of this element by German-Swedish chemist [[Carl Wilhelm Scheele]].
*[[1798]] – [[French Revolutionary Wars]]: [[Battle of the Nile|Battle of the Nile (Battle of Aboukir Bay)]]: Battle begins when a British fleet engages the French Revolutionary Navy fleet in an unusual night action.<ref>{{cite web |title=Battle of the Nile {{!}} Egyptian-European history |url=https://www.britannica.com/event/Battle-of-the-Nile |website=Encyclopedia Britannica |access-date=15 December 2020 |language=en}}</ref>
*[[1800]] – The [[Acts of Union 1800]] are passed which merge the [[Kingdom of Great Britain]] and the [[Kingdom of Ireland]] into the [[United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland]].
*[[1801]] – [[First Barbary War]]: The American [[schooner]] {{USS|Enterprise|1799|6}} [[Action of 1 August 1801|captures]] the [[Tripoli, Libya|Tripoli]]tan [[polacca]] ''Tripoli'' in a [[single-ship action]] off the coast of modern-day [[Libya]].
*[[1834]] – [[Abolitionism in the United Kingdom|Slavery is abolished in the British Empire]] as the [[Slavery Abolition Act 1833]] comes into force, although it remains legal in the possessions of the [[East India Company]] until the passage of the [[Indian Slavery Act, 1843]].
*  1834   – Construction begins on the [[Wilberforce Monument]] in [[Kingston Upon Hull]].<ref name=Wilberforce>{{Cite web|last=Kitching|first=Sophie|date=9 February 2018|title=The bizarre reason this Hull landmark was moved 82 years ago|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/news/history/bizarre-reason-hull-landmark-moved-1176719|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210302144359/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/news/history/bizarre-reason-hull-landmark-moved-1176719|archive-date=2 March 2021|access-date=1 October 2021|website=[[Hull Daily Mail]]|publisher=[[Trinity Mirror]]|language=en}}</ref>
*[[1842]] – The [[Lombard Street riot]] erupts in [[Philadelphia]], [[Pennsylvania]], United States.
*[[1849]] – ''[[Joven Daniel]]'' wrecks at the coast of [[Araucanía (historic region)|Araucanía]], Chile, leading to allegations that local [[Mapuche]] tribes murdered survivors and kidnapped [[Elisa Bravo]].<ref name=Munoz2010>{{Cite journal|title=El naufragio del bergantín Joven Daniel, 1849. El indígena en el imaginario histórico de Chile|journal=Tiempo Histórico|last=Muñoz Sougarret|first=Jorge|issue=1|year=2010|language=es|pages=133–148}}</ref>
*[[1855]] – The first ascent of [[Monte Rosa]], the second highest summit in the Alps.
*[[1863]] – At the suggestion of Senator [[Johan Vilhelm Snellman|J. V. Snellman]] and the order of Emperor [[Alexander II of Russia|Alexander II]], full rights were promised to the [[Finnish language]] by a language regulation in the [[Grand Duchy of Finland]].<ref>{{cite book| author= Olavi Junnila | title= Suomen historia 5 | year= 1986 | chapter= Autonomian rakentaminen ja kansallisen nousun aika | page= 151 | location = Helsinki | publisher= Weilin + Göös | isbn= 951-35-2494-9 | language = fi}}</ref>
*[[1876]] – [[Colorado]] is admitted as the 38th [[U.S. state]].
*[[1893]] – [[Henry Perky]] patents [[shredded wheat]].
*[[1894]] – The [[Empire of Japan]] and [[Qing dynasty|Qing China]] declare war on each other after a week of fighting over Korea, formally inaugurating the [[First Sino-Japanese War]].
===1901–present===
*[[1907]] – The start of the [[Brownsea Island Scout camp|first Scout camp]] on [[Brownsea Island]], the origin of the worldwide [[Scouting]] movement.
*[[1911]] – [[Harriet Quimby]] takes her pilot's test and becomes the first U.S. woman to earn an Aero Club of America aviator's certificate.
*[[1914]] – The [[German Empire]] declares war on the [[Russian Empire]] at the opening of [[World War I]]. The [[Military of Switzerland|Swiss Army]] mobilizes because of World War I.
*[[1927]] – The [[Nanchang Uprising]] marks the first significant battle in the [[Chinese Civil War]] between the [[Kuomintang]] and [[Chinese Communist Party]]. This day is commemorated as the anniversary of the founding of the [[People's Liberation Army]].
*[[1933]] – Anti-Fascist activists [[Bruno Tesch (antifascist)|Bruno Tesch]], Walter Möller, Karl Wolff and August Lütgens are executed by the Nazi regime in [[Altona, Hamburg|Altona]].
*[[1936]] – The [[1936 Summer Olympics|Olympics]] opened in [[Berlin]] with a ceremony presided over by [[Adolf Hitler]].
*[[1937]] – [[Josip Broz Tito]] reads the resolution "Manifesto of constitutional congress of KPH" to the constitutive congress of KPH ([[League of Communists of Croatia|Croatian Communist Party]]) in woods near [[Samobor]].
*[[1943]] – [[World War II]]: [[Operation Tidal Wave]] also known as "Black Sunday", was a failed American attempt to destroy Romanian oil fields.
*[[1944]] – World War II: The [[Warsaw Uprising]] against the [[Nazism|Nazi German]] occupation breaks out in [[Warsaw]], Poland.
*[[1946]] – Leaders of the [[Russian Liberation Army]], a force of Russian prisoners of war that [[Collaboration with the Axis Powers during World War II|collaborated with Nazi Germany]], are executed in Moscow, [[Soviet Union]] for treason.
*[[1950]] – [[Guam]] is organized as an unincorporated territory of the United States as the President [[Harry S. Truman]] signs the [[Guam Organic Act of 1950|Guam Organic Act]].<ref>{{cite book|author=Department of the Interior|title=Years of Progress, 1945–1952|location=Washington, D.C.|publisher=U.S. Department of the Interior|date=1953|oclc=2183193|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=l3CGAAAAMAAJ|pages=103–104}}</ref>
*[[1957]] – The United States and Canada form the [[North American Aerospace Defense Command]] (NORAD).
*[[1960]] – [[Dahomey]] (later renamed [[Benin]]) declares independence from France.
*[[1960]] – [[Islamabad]] is declared the [[federal capital]] of the [[Government of Pakistan]].
*[[1961]] – U.S. Defense Secretary [[Robert McNamara]] orders the creation of the [[Defense Intelligence Agency]] (DIA), the nation's first centralized military espionage organization.
*[[1964]] – The former [[Belgian Congo]] is renamed the [[Democratic Republic of the Congo]].
*[[1965]] – [[Frank Herbert]]'s novel, [[Dune (novel)|''Dune'']] was published for the first time.  It was named as the world's best-selling science fiction novel in 2003.
*[[1966]] – [[Charles Whitman]] kills 16 people at the [[University of Texas at Austin]] before being killed by the police.
*  1966   – Purges of intellectuals and imperialists becomes official China policy at the beginning of the [[Cultural Revolution]].
*[[1968]] – The coronation is held of [[Hassanal Bolkiah]], the 29th [[Sultan of Brunei]].
*[[1971]] – [[The Concert for Bangladesh]], organized by former Beatle George Harrison, is held at Madison Square Garden in New York City.
*[[1974]] – [[Cyprus dispute]]: The [[United Nations Security Council]] authorizes the [[United Nations Peacekeeping Force in Cyprus|UNFICYP]] to create the "[[United Nations Buffer Zone in Cyprus|Green Line]]", dividing [[Cyprus]] into two zones.
*[[1976]] – [[Niki Lauda]] has a severe accident that almost claims his life at the [[1976 German Grand Prix|German Grand Prix]] at [[Nürburgring|Nurburgring]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=1976 German Grand Prix {{!}} Motorsport Database|url=https://www.motorsportmagazine.com/database/races/1976-german-grand-prix/|access-date=2021-05-17|website=Motorsport Database - Motor Sport Magazine|language=en-GB}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last=Saward|first=Joe|date=2020-10-08|title=How the 1976 German Grand Prix Forever Changed the Fate of the Nurburgring Nordschleife|url=https://www.autoweek.com/racing/formula-1/a34313295/1976-german-grand-prix-changed-fate-nurburgring-nordschleife/|access-date=2021-05-17|website=Autoweek|language=en-US}}</ref>
*[[1980]] – [[Vigdís Finnbogadóttir]] is elected [[President of Iceland]] and becomes the world's first democratically elected female head of state.
*  1980   – A [[Buttevant Rail Disaster|train crash]] kills 18 people in [[County Cork]], [[Republic of Ireland|Ireland]].
*[[1981]] – [[MTV]] begins broadcasting in the United States and airs its first video, "[[Video Killed the Radio Star]]" by [[The Buggles]].
*[[1984]] – Commercial [[peat]]-cutters discover the preserved [[bog body]] of a man, called [[Lindow Man]], at [[Lindow Moss]], [[Cheshire]], England.
*[[1988]] – A British soldier was killed in the [[Inglis Barracks bombing]] in London, England.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://cain.ulst.ac.uk/othelem/chron/ch88.htm#Aug|title=CAIN: Chronology of the Conflict 1988|first=Dr Martin|last=Melaugh|website=Cain.ulst.ac.uk|access-date=4 August 2017}}</ref>
*[[1993]] – The [[Great Mississippi and Missouri Rivers Flood of 1993]] comes to a peak.
*[[2004]] – A [[Ycuá Bolaños supermarket fire|supermarket fire]] kills 396 people and injures 500 others in [[Asunción, Paraguay]].
*[[2007]] – The [[I-35W Mississippi River bridge]] spanning the [[Mississippi River]] in [[Minneapolis|Minneapolis, Minnesota]], collapses during the evening [[rush hour]], killing 13 people and injuring 145.
*[[2008]] – The [[Beijing–Tianjin Intercity Railway]] begins operation as the fastest commuter rail system in the world.
*  2008   – Eleven mountaineers from international expeditions died on K2, the second-highest mountain on Earth in [[2008 K2 disaster|the worst single accident in the history of K2 mountaineering]].
*[[2017]] – A [[suicide attack]] on a mosque in Herat, [[Afghanistan]] kills 20 people.

*[[Emancipation Day]] is commemorated in many parts of the former British Empire, which marks the day the [[Slavery Abolition Act 1833]] came into effect which abolished [[Chattel Slavery|chattel slavery]] in the [[British Empire]]:<ref>{{cite book|author1-link=Jeffrey Kerr-Ritchie |last1=Kerr-Ritchie |first1=Jeffrey R. · |title=Rites of August First Emancipation Day in the Black Atlantic World |date=2011 |isbn=9780807143643 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=-aPYCwAAQBAJ}}</ref>

**Earliest day on which Emancipation Day can fall, where it is commemorated on the first Monday of August, for example in [[Anguilla]], [[the Bahamas]], [[British Virgin Islands]]
*Earliest day on which [[Caribana]] carnival can fall, takes place on the first Weekend of August. ([[Toronto]])

*[[Swiss National Day]] ([[Switzerland]])
*The beginning of [[autumn]] observances in the Northern hemisphere and [[Spring (season)|spring]] observances in the Southern hemisphere ([[Wheel of the Year|Neopagan Wheel of the Year]]):
**[[Lughnasadh]] in the Northern hemisphere, [[Imbolc]] in the Southern hemisphere; traditionally begins on the eve of August 1. ([[Gaels]], [[Ireland]], [[Scotland]], [[Neopagans]])
**[[Lammas]] ([[England]], [[Scotland]], [[Neopagans]])
**[[Pachamama Raymi]] ([[Quechuan]] in [[Ecuador]] and [[Peru]])
*The first day of [[Carnaval del Pueblo]] ([[Burgess Park]], [[London]], England)
*[[Victory Day]] ([[Cambodia]], [[Laos]], [[Vietnam]])
*[[World Scout Scarf Day]]
*[[Yorkshire Day]] ([[Yorkshire]], [[England]])

<div class="shortdescription nomobile noexcerpt noprint searchaux" style="display:none">Day of the year</div><p><b>August 1</b> is the 213th day of the year (214th in <a href="/wiki/Leap_year" title="Leap year">leap years</a>) in the <a href="/wiki/Gregorian_calendar" title="Gregorian calendar">Gregorian calendar</a>;  152 days remain until the end of the year.
</p><meta property="mw:PageProp/toc" />
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Events">Events</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=August_1&action=edit&section=1" title="Edit section: Events"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Pre-1600">Pre-1600</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=August_1&action=edit&section=2" title="Edit section: Pre-1600"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<ul><li><a href="/wiki/30_BC" title="30 BC">30 BC</a> – <a href="/wiki/Augustus" title="Augustus">Octavian</a> (later known as Augustus) enters <a href="/wiki/Alexandria" title="Alexandria">Alexandria</a>, <a href="/wiki/Egypt" title="Egypt">Egypt</a>, bringing it under the control of the <a href="/wiki/Roman_Republic" title="Roman Republic">Roman Republic</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/AD_69" title="AD 69">AD 69</a> – <a href="/wiki/Revolt_of_the_Batavi" title="Revolt of the Batavi">Batavian rebellion</a>: The <a href="/wiki/Batavi_(Germanic_tribe)" title="Batavi (Germanic tribe)">Batavians</a> in <a href="/wiki/Germania_Inferior" title="Germania Inferior">Germania Inferior</a> (Netherlands) revolt under the leadership of <a href="/wiki/Gaius_Julius_Civilis" title="Gaius Julius Civilis">Gaius Julius Civilis</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/527" title="527">527</a> – <a href="/wiki/Justinian_I" title="Justinian I">Justinian I</a> becomes the sole ruler of the <a href="/wiki/Byzantine_Empire" title="Byzantine Empire">Byzantine Empire</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/607" title="607">607</a> – <a href="/wiki/Ono_no_Imoko" title="Ono no Imoko">Ono no Imoko</a> is dispatched as envoy to the <a href="/wiki/Sui_dynasty" title="Sui dynasty">Sui</a> court in China (Traditional <a href="/wiki/Japanese_calendar" title="Japanese calendar">Japanese date</a>: July 3, 607).</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/902" title="902">902</a> – <a href="/wiki/Taormina" title="Taormina">Taormina</a>, the last Byzantine stronghold in <a href="/wiki/Sicily" title="Sicily">Sicily</a>, is <a href="/wiki/Siege_of_Taormina_(902)" title="Siege of Taormina (902)">captured</a> by the <a href="/wiki/Aghlabid" class="mw-redirect" title="Aghlabid">Aghlabid</a> army, concluding the <a href="/wiki/Muslim_conquest_of_Sicily" title="Muslim conquest of Sicily">Muslim conquest of Sicily</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-1" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-1"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>1<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1203" title="1203">1203</a> – <a href="/wiki/Isaac_II_Angelos" title="Isaac II Angelos">Isaac II Angelos</a>, restored <a href="/wiki/Byzantine_Emperor" class="mw-redirect" title="Byzantine Emperor">Byzantine Emperor</a>, declares his son <a href="/wiki/Alexios_IV_Angelos" title="Alexios IV Angelos">Alexios IV Angelos</a> co-emperor after pressure from the forces of the <a href="/wiki/Fourth_Crusade" title="Fourth Crusade">Fourth Crusade</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1291" title="1291">1291</a> – The <a href="/wiki/Old_Swiss_Confederacy" title="Old Swiss Confederacy">Old Swiss Confederacy</a> is formed with the signature of the <a href="/wiki/Federal_Charter_of_1291" title="Federal Charter of 1291">Federal Charter</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1469" title="1469">1469</a> – <a href="/wiki/Louis_XI_of_France" class="mw-redirect" title="Louis XI of France">Louis XI of France</a> founds the <a href="/wiki/Chivalric_order" class="mw-redirect" title="Chivalric order">chivalric order</a> called the <a href="/wiki/Order_of_Saint_Michael" title="Order of Saint Michael">Order of Saint Michael</a> in <a href="/wiki/Amboise" title="Amboise">Amboise</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1498" title="1498">1498</a> – <a href="/wiki/Christopher_Columbus" title="Christopher Columbus">Christopher Columbus</a> becomes the first European to visit what is now <a href="/wiki/Venezuela" title="Venezuela">Venezuela</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1571" title="1571">1571</a> – The <a href="/wiki/Ottoman%E2%80%93Venetian_War_(1570%E2%80%9373)#Ottoman_conquest_of_Cyprus" class="mw-redirect" title="Ottoman–Venetian War (1570–73)">Ottoman conquest of Cyprus</a> is concluded, by the surrender of <a href="/wiki/Famagusta" title="Famagusta">Famagusta</a>.</li></ul>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="1601–1900"><span id="1601.E2.80.931900"></span>1601–1900</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=August_1&action=edit&section=3" title="Edit section: 1601–1900"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<ul><li><a href="/wiki/1620" title="1620">1620</a> – <a href="/wiki/Speedwell_(1577_ship)" title="Speedwell (1577 ship)"><i>Speedwell</i></a> leaves <a href="/wiki/Delfshaven" title="Delfshaven">Delfshaven</a> to bring <a href="/wiki/Pilgrim_Fathers" class="mw-redirect" title="Pilgrim Fathers">pilgrims</a> to <a href="/wiki/Plymouth_Colony" title="Plymouth Colony">America</a> by way of England.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1664" title="1664">1664</a> – <a href="/wiki/Ottoman_Empire" title="Ottoman Empire">Ottoman forces</a> are defeated in the <a href="/wiki/Battle_of_Saint_Gotthard_(1664)" title="Battle of Saint Gotthard (1664)">battle of Saint Gotthard</a> by an <a href="/wiki/Habsburg_monarchy" title="Habsburg monarchy">Austrian</a> army led by <a href="/wiki/Raimondo_Montecuccoli" title="Raimondo Montecuccoli">Raimondo Montecuccoli</a>, resulting in the <a href="/wiki/Peace_of_Vasv%C3%A1r" title="Peace of Vasvár">Peace of Vasvár</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1714" title="1714">1714</a> – George, Elector of <a href="/wiki/Hanover" title="Hanover">Hanover</a>, becomes King <a href="/wiki/George_I_of_Great_Britain" title="George I of Great Britain">George I of Great Britain</a>, marking the beginning of the <a href="/wiki/Georgian_era" title="Georgian era">Georgian era</a> of British history.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1759" title="1759">1759</a> – <a href="/wiki/Seven_Years%27_War" title="Seven Years' War">Seven Years' War</a>: The <a href="/wiki/Battle_of_Minden" title="Battle of Minden">Battle of Minden</a>, an allied Anglo-German army victory over the French. In Britain this was one of a number of events that constituted the <a href="/wiki/Annus_Mirabilis_of_1759" class="mw-redirect" title="Annus Mirabilis of 1759">Annus Mirabilis of 1759</a> and is celebrated as <a href="/wiki/Minden_Day" title="Minden Day">Minden Day</a> by certain British Army regiments.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1774" title="1774">1774</a> – British scientist <a href="/wiki/Joseph_Priestley" title="Joseph Priestley">Joseph Priestley</a> discovers <a href="/wiki/Oxygen" title="Oxygen">oxygen</a> gas, corroborating the prior discovery of this element by German-Swedish chemist <a href="/wiki/Carl_Wilhelm_Scheele" title="Carl Wilhelm Scheele">Carl Wilhelm Scheele</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1798" title="1798">1798</a> – <a href="/wiki/French_Revolutionary_Wars" title="French Revolutionary Wars">French Revolutionary Wars</a>: <a href="/wiki/Battle_of_the_Nile" title="Battle of the Nile">Battle of the Nile (Battle of Aboukir Bay)</a>: Battle begins when a British fleet engages the French Revolutionary Navy fleet in an unusual night action.<sup id="cite_ref-2" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-2"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>2<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1800" title="1800">1800</a> – The <a href="/wiki/Acts_of_Union_1800" title="Acts of Union 1800">Acts of Union 1800</a> are passed which merge the <a href="/wiki/Kingdom_of_Great_Britain" title="Kingdom of Great Britain">Kingdom of Great Britain</a> and the <a href="/wiki/Kingdom_of_Ireland" title="Kingdom of Ireland">Kingdom of Ireland</a> into the <a href="/wiki/United_Kingdom_of_Great_Britain_and_Ireland" title="United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland">United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1801" title="1801">1801</a> – <a href="/wiki/First_Barbary_War" title="First Barbary War">First Barbary War</a>: The American <a href="/wiki/Schooner" title="Schooner">schooner</a> <a href="/wiki/USS_Enterprise_(1799)" title="USS Enterprise (1799)">USS <i>Enterprise</i></a> <a href="/wiki/Action_of_1_August_1801" title="Action of 1 August 1801">captures</a> the <a href="/wiki/Tripoli,_Libya" title="Tripoli, Libya">Tripolitan</a> <a href="/wiki/Polacca" title="Polacca">polacca</a> <i>Tripoli</i> in a <a href="/wiki/Single-ship_action" class="mw-redirect" title="Single-ship action">single-ship action</a> off the coast of modern-day <a href="/wiki/Libya" title="Libya">Libya</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1834" title="1834">1834</a> – <a href="/wiki/Abolitionism_in_the_United_Kingdom" title="Abolitionism in the United Kingdom">Slavery is abolished in the British Empire</a> as the <a href="/wiki/Slavery_Abolition_Act_1833" title="Slavery Abolition Act 1833">Slavery Abolition Act 1833</a> comes into force, although it remains legal in the possessions of the <a href="/wiki/East_India_Company" title="East India Company">East India Company</a> until the passage of the <a href="/wiki/Indian_Slavery_Act,_1843" title="Indian Slavery Act, 1843">Indian Slavery Act, 1843</a>.</li>
<li>1834   – Construction begins on the <a href="/wiki/Wilberforce_Monument" title="Wilberforce Monument">Wilberforce Monument</a> in <a href="/wiki/Kingston_Upon_Hull" class="mw-redirect" title="Kingston Upon Hull">Kingston Upon Hull</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-Wilberforce_3-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Wilberforce-3"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>3<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1842" title="1842">1842</a> – The <a href="/wiki/Lombard_Street_riot" title="Lombard Street riot">Lombard Street riot</a> erupts in <a href="/wiki/Philadelphia" title="Philadelphia">Philadelphia</a>, <a href="/wiki/Pennsylvania" title="Pennsylvania">Pennsylvania</a>, United States.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1849" title="1849">1849</a> – <i><a href="/wiki/Joven_Daniel" title="Joven Daniel">Joven Daniel</a></i> wrecks at the coast of <a href="/wiki/Araucan%C3%ADa_(historic_region)" title="Araucanía (historic region)">Araucanía</a>, Chile, leading to allegations that local <a href="/wiki/Mapuche" title="Mapuche">Mapuche</a> tribes murdered survivors and kidnapped <a href="/wiki/Elisa_Bravo" title="Elisa Bravo">Elisa Bravo</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-Munoz2010_4-0" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Munoz2010-4"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>4<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1855" title="1855">1855</a> – The first ascent of <a href="/wiki/Monte_Rosa" title="Monte Rosa">Monte Rosa</a>, the second highest summit in the Alps.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1863" title="1863">1863</a> – At the suggestion of Senator <a href="/wiki/Johan_Vilhelm_Snellman" title="Johan Vilhelm Snellman">J. V. Snellman</a> and the order of Emperor <a href="/wiki/Alexander_II_of_Russia" title="Alexander II of Russia">Alexander II</a>, full rights were promised to the <a href="/wiki/Finnish_language" title="Finnish language">Finnish language</a> by a language regulation in the <a href="/wiki/Grand_Duchy_of_Finland" title="Grand Duchy of Finland">Grand Duchy of Finland</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-5" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-5"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>5<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1876" title="1876">1876</a> – <a href="/wiki/Colorado" title="Colorado">Colorado</a> is admitted as the 38th <a href="/wiki/U.S._state" title="U.S. state">U.S. state</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1893" title="1893">1893</a> – <a href="/wiki/Henry_Perky" title="Henry Perky">Henry Perky</a> patents <a href="/wiki/Shredded_wheat" title="Shredded wheat">shredded wheat</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1894" title="1894">1894</a> – The <a href="/wiki/Empire_of_Japan" title="Empire of Japan">Empire of Japan</a> and <a href="/wiki/Qing_dynasty" title="Qing dynasty">Qing China</a> declare war on each other after a week of fighting over Korea, formally inaugurating the <a href="/wiki/First_Sino-Japanese_War" title="First Sino-Japanese War">First Sino-Japanese War</a>.</li></ul>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="1901–present"><span id="1901.E2.80.93present"></span>1901–present</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=August_1&action=edit&section=4" title="Edit section: 1901–present"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<ul><li><a href="/wiki/1907" title="1907">1907</a> – The start of the <a href="/wiki/Brownsea_Island_Scout_camp" title="Brownsea Island Scout camp">first Scout camp</a> on <a href="/wiki/Brownsea_Island" title="Brownsea Island">Brownsea Island</a>, the origin of the worldwide <a href="/wiki/Scouting" title="Scouting">Scouting</a> movement.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1911" title="1911">1911</a> – <a href="/wiki/Harriet_Quimby" title="Harriet Quimby">Harriet Quimby</a> takes her pilot's test and becomes the first U.S. woman to earn an Aero Club of America aviator's certificate.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1914" title="1914">1914</a> – The <a href="/wiki/German_Empire" title="German Empire">German Empire</a> declares war on the <a href="/wiki/Russian_Empire" title="Russian Empire">Russian Empire</a> at the opening of <a href="/wiki/World_War_I" title="World War I">World War I</a>. The <a href="/wiki/Military_of_Switzerland" class="mw-redirect" title="Military of Switzerland">Swiss Army</a> mobilizes because of World War I.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1927" title="1927">1927</a> – The <a href="/wiki/Nanchang_Uprising" class="mw-redirect" title="Nanchang Uprising">Nanchang Uprising</a> marks the first significant battle in the <a href="/wiki/Chinese_Civil_War" title="Chinese Civil War">Chinese Civil War</a> between the <a href="/wiki/Kuomintang" title="Kuomintang">Kuomintang</a> and <a href="/wiki/Chinese_Communist_Party" title="Chinese Communist Party">Chinese Communist Party</a>. This day is commemorated as the anniversary of the founding of the <a href="/wiki/People%27s_Liberation_Army" title="People's Liberation Army">People's Liberation Army</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1933" title="1933">1933</a> – Anti-Fascist activists <a href="/wiki/Bruno_Tesch_(antifascist)" title="Bruno Tesch (antifascist)">Bruno Tesch</a>, Walter Möller, Karl Wolff and August Lütgens are executed by the Nazi regime in <a href="/wiki/Altona,_Hamburg" title="Altona, Hamburg">Altona</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1936" title="1936">1936</a> – The <a href="/wiki/1936_Summer_Olympics" title="1936 Summer Olympics">Olympics</a> opened in <a href="/wiki/Berlin" title="Berlin">Berlin</a> with a ceremony presided over by <a href="/wiki/Adolf_Hitler" title="Adolf Hitler">Adolf Hitler</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1937" title="1937">1937</a> – <a href="/wiki/Josip_Broz_Tito" title="Josip Broz Tito">Josip Broz Tito</a> reads the resolution "Manifesto of constitutional congress of KPH" to the constitutive congress of KPH (<a href="/wiki/League_of_Communists_of_Croatia" title="League of Communists of Croatia">Croatian Communist Party</a>) in woods near <a href="/wiki/Samobor" title="Samobor">Samobor</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1943" title="1943">1943</a> – <a href="/wiki/World_War_II" title="World War II">World War II</a>: <a href="/wiki/Operation_Tidal_Wave" title="Operation Tidal Wave">Operation Tidal Wave</a> also known as "Black Sunday", was a failed American attempt to destroy Romanian oil fields.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1944" title="1944">1944</a> – World War II: The <a href="/wiki/Warsaw_Uprising" title="Warsaw Uprising">Warsaw Uprising</a> against the <a href="/wiki/Nazism" title="Nazism">Nazi German</a> occupation breaks out in <a href="/wiki/Warsaw" title="Warsaw">Warsaw</a>, Poland.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1946" title="1946">1946</a> – Leaders of the <a href="/wiki/Russian_Liberation_Army" title="Russian Liberation Army">Russian Liberation Army</a>, a force of Russian prisoners of war that <a href="/wiki/Collaboration_with_the_Axis_Powers_during_World_War_II" class="mw-redirect mw-disambig" title="Collaboration with the Axis Powers during World War II">collaborated with Nazi Germany</a>, are executed in Moscow, <a href="/wiki/Soviet_Union" title="Soviet Union">Soviet Union</a> for treason.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1950" title="1950">1950</a> – <a href="/wiki/Guam" title="Guam">Guam</a> is organized as an unincorporated territory of the United States as the President <a href="/wiki/Harry_S._Truman" title="Harry S. Truman">Harry S. Truman</a> signs the <a href="/wiki/Guam_Organic_Act_of_1950" title="Guam Organic Act of 1950">Guam Organic Act</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-6" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-6"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>6<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1957" title="1957">1957</a> – The United States and Canada form the <a href="/wiki/North_American_Aerospace_Defense_Command" class="mw-redirect" title="North American Aerospace Defense Command">North American Aerospace Defense Command</a> (NORAD).</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1960" title="1960">1960</a> – <a href="/wiki/Dahomey" title="Dahomey">Dahomey</a> (later renamed <a href="/wiki/Benin" title="Benin">Benin</a>) declares independence from France.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1960" title="1960">1960</a> – <a href="/wiki/Islamabad" title="Islamabad">Islamabad</a> is declared the <a href="/wiki/Federal_capital" title="Federal capital">federal capital</a> of the <a href="/wiki/Government_of_Pakistan" title="Government of Pakistan">Government of Pakistan</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1961" title="1961">1961</a> – U.S. Defense Secretary <a href="/wiki/Robert_McNamara" title="Robert McNamara">Robert McNamara</a> orders the creation of the <a href="/wiki/Defense_Intelligence_Agency" title="Defense Intelligence Agency">Defense Intelligence Agency</a> (DIA), the nation's first centralized military espionage organization.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1964" title="1964">1964</a> – The former <a href="/wiki/Belgian_Congo" title="Belgian Congo">Belgian Congo</a> is renamed the <a href="/wiki/Democratic_Republic_of_the_Congo" title="Democratic Republic of the Congo">Democratic Republic of the Congo</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1965" title="1965">1965</a> – <a href="/wiki/Frank_Herbert" title="Frank Herbert">Frank Herbert</a>'s novel, <a href="/wiki/Dune_(novel)" title="Dune (novel)"><i>Dune</i></a> was published for the first time.  It was named as the world's best-selling science fiction novel in 2003.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1966" title="1966">1966</a> – <a href="/wiki/Charles_Whitman" title="Charles Whitman">Charles Whitman</a> kills 16 people at the <a href="/wiki/University_of_Texas_at_Austin" title="University of Texas at Austin">University of Texas at Austin</a> before being killed by the police.</li>
<li>1966   – Purges of intellectuals and imperialists becomes official China policy at the beginning of the <a href="/wiki/Cultural_Revolution" title="Cultural Revolution">Cultural Revolution</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1968" title="1968">1968</a> – The coronation is held of <a href="/wiki/Hassanal_Bolkiah" title="Hassanal Bolkiah">Hassanal Bolkiah</a>, the 29th <a href="/wiki/Sultan_of_Brunei" class="mw-redirect" title="Sultan of Brunei">Sultan of Brunei</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1971" title="1971">1971</a> – <a href="/wiki/The_Concert_for_Bangladesh" title="The Concert for Bangladesh">The Concert for Bangladesh</a>, organized by former Beatle George Harrison, is held at Madison Square Garden in New York City.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1974" title="1974">1974</a> – <a href="/wiki/Cyprus_dispute" class="mw-redirect" title="Cyprus dispute">Cyprus dispute</a>: The <a href="/wiki/United_Nations_Security_Council" title="United Nations Security Council">United Nations Security Council</a> authorizes the <a href="/wiki/United_Nations_Peacekeeping_Force_in_Cyprus" title="United Nations Peacekeeping Force in Cyprus">UNFICYP</a> to create the "<a href="/wiki/United_Nations_Buffer_Zone_in_Cyprus" title="United Nations Buffer Zone in Cyprus">Green Line</a>", dividing <a href="/wiki/Cyprus" title="Cyprus">Cyprus</a> into two zones.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1976" title="1976">1976</a> – <a href="/wiki/Niki_Lauda" title="Niki Lauda">Niki Lauda</a> has a severe accident that almost claims his life at the <a href="/wiki/1976_German_Grand_Prix" title="1976 German Grand Prix">German Grand Prix</a> at <a href="/wiki/N%C3%BCrburgring" title="Nürburgring">Nurburgring</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-7" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-7"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>7<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup><sup id="cite_ref-8" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-8"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>8<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1980" title="1980">1980</a> – <a href="/wiki/Vigd%C3%ADs_Finnbogad%C3%B3ttir" title="Vigdís Finnbogadóttir">Vigdís Finnbogadóttir</a> is elected <a href="/wiki/President_of_Iceland" title="President of Iceland">President of Iceland</a> and becomes the world's first democratically elected female head of state.</li>
<li>1980   – A <a href="/wiki/Buttevant_Rail_Disaster" title="Buttevant Rail Disaster">train crash</a> kills 18 people in <a href="/wiki/County_Cork" title="County Cork">County Cork</a>, <a href="/wiki/Republic_of_Ireland" title="Republic of Ireland">Ireland</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1981" title="1981">1981</a> – <a href="/wiki/MTV" title="MTV">MTV</a> begins broadcasting in the United States and airs its first video, "<a href="/wiki/Video_Killed_the_Radio_Star" title="Video Killed the Radio Star">Video Killed the Radio Star</a>" by <a href="/wiki/The_Buggles" title="The Buggles">The Buggles</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1984" title="1984">1984</a> – Commercial <a href="/wiki/Peat" title="Peat">peat</a>-cutters discover the preserved <a href="/wiki/Bog_body" title="Bog body">bog body</a> of a man, called <a href="/wiki/Lindow_Man" title="Lindow Man">Lindow Man</a>, at <a href="/wiki/Lindow_Moss" title="Lindow Moss">Lindow Moss</a>, <a href="/wiki/Cheshire" title="Cheshire">Cheshire</a>, England.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1988" title="1988">1988</a> – A British soldier was killed in the <a href="/wiki/Inglis_Barracks_bombing" class="mw-redirect" title="Inglis Barracks bombing">Inglis Barracks bombing</a> in London, England.<sup id="cite_ref-9" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-9"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>9<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/1993" title="1993">1993</a> – The <a href="/wiki/Great_Mississippi_and_Missouri_Rivers_Flood_of_1993" class="mw-redirect" title="Great Mississippi and Missouri Rivers Flood of 1993">Great Mississippi and Missouri Rivers Flood of 1993</a> comes to a peak.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/2004" title="2004">2004</a> – A <a href="/wiki/Ycu%C3%A1_Bola%C3%B1os_supermarket_fire" title="Ycuá Bolaños supermarket fire">supermarket fire</a> kills 396 people and injures 500 others in <a href="/wiki/Asunci%C3%B3n,_Paraguay" class="mw-redirect" title="Asunción, Paraguay">Asunción, Paraguay</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/2007" title="2007">2007</a> – The <a href="/wiki/I-35W_Mississippi_River_bridge" title="I-35W Mississippi River bridge">I-35W Mississippi River bridge</a> spanning the <a href="/wiki/Mississippi_River" title="Mississippi River">Mississippi River</a> in <a href="/wiki/Minneapolis" title="Minneapolis">Minneapolis, Minnesota</a>, collapses during the evening <a href="/wiki/Rush_hour" title="Rush hour">rush hour</a>, killing 13 people and injuring 145.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/2008" title="2008">2008</a> – The <a href="/wiki/Beijing%E2%80%93Tianjin_Intercity_Railway" class="mw-redirect" title="Beijing–Tianjin Intercity Railway">Beijing–Tianjin Intercity Railway</a> begins operation as the fastest commuter rail system in the world.</li>
<li>2008   – Eleven mountaineers from international expeditions died on K2, the second-highest mountain on Earth in <a href="/wiki/2008_K2_disaster" title="2008 K2 disaster">the worst single accident in the history of K2 mountaineering</a>.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/2017" title="2017">2017</a> – A <a href="/wiki/Suicide_attack" title="Suicide attack">suicide attack</a> on a mosque in Herat, <a href="/wiki/Afghanistan" title="Afghanistan">Afghanistan</a> kills 20 people.</li></ul>

<li><a href="/wiki/1555" title="1555">1555</a> – <a href="/wiki/Edward_Kelley" title="Edward Kelley">Edward Kelley</a>, English spirit medium (d. 1597)</li>

<li><a href="/wiki/1744" title="1744">1744</a> – <a href="/wiki/Jean-Baptiste_Lamarck" title="Jean-Baptiste Lamarck">Jean-Baptiste Lamarck</a>, French soldier, biologist, and academic (d. 1829)</li>

<li><a href="/wiki/1779" title="1779">1779</a> – <a href="/wiki/Francis_Scott_Key" title="Francis Scott Key">Francis Scott Key</a>, American lawyer, author, and poet (d. 1843)</li>

<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Holidays_and_observances">Holidays and observances</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=August_1&action=edit&section=13" title="Edit section: Holidays and observances"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<ul><li><a href="/wiki/Armed_Forces_Day_(Lebanon)" class="mw-redirect" title="Armed Forces Day (Lebanon)">Armed Forces Day (Lebanon)</a></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Armed_Forces_Day_(China)" class="mw-redirect" title="Armed Forces Day (China)">Armed Forces Day (China)</a> or Anniversary of the Founding of the <a href="/wiki/People%27s_Liberation_Army" title="People's Liberation Army">People's Liberation Army</a> (People's Republic of China)</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Azerbaijani_Language_and_Alphabet_Day" class="mw-redirect" title="Azerbaijani Language and Alphabet Day">Azerbaijani Language and Alphabet Day</a> (<a href="/wiki/Azerbaijan" title="Azerbaijan">Azerbaijan</a>)</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Emancipation_Day" title="Emancipation Day">Emancipation Day</a> is commemorated in many parts of the former British Empire, which marks the day the <a href="/wiki/Slavery_Abolition_Act_1833" title="Slavery Abolition Act 1833">Slavery Abolition Act 1833</a> came into effect which abolished <a href="/wiki/Chattel_Slavery" class="mw-redirect" title="Chattel Slavery">chattel slavery</a> in the <a href="/wiki/British_Empire" title="British Empire">British Empire</a>:<sup id="cite_ref-40" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-40"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>40<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
<ul><li>Emancipation Day is a public holiday in <a href="/wiki/Barbados" title="Barbados">Barbados</a>, <a href="/wiki/Bermuda" title="Bermuda">Bermuda</a>, <a href="/wiki/Guyana" title="Guyana">Guyana</a>, <a href="/wiki/Jamaica" title="Jamaica">Jamaica</a>, <a href="/wiki/Saint_Vincent_and_the_Grenadines" title="Saint Vincent and the Grenadines">Saint Vincent and the Grenadines</a>, <a href="/wiki/Trinidad_and_Tobago" title="Trinidad and Tobago">Trinidad and Tobago</a></li>
<li>Earliest day on which Emancipation Day can fall, where it is commemorated on the first Monday of August, for example in <a href="/wiki/Anguilla" title="Anguilla">Anguilla</a>, <a href="/wiki/The_Bahamas" title="The Bahamas">the Bahamas</a>, <a href="/wiki/British_Virgin_Islands" title="British Virgin Islands">British Virgin Islands</a></li></ul></li>
<li>Earliest day on which <a href="/wiki/Caribana" title="Caribana">Caribana</a> carnival can fall, takes place on the first Weekend of August. (<a href="/wiki/Toronto" title="Toronto">Toronto</a>)</li>
<li>Christian <a href="/wiki/Calendar_of_saints" title="Calendar of saints">feast day</a>:
<ul><li><a href="/wiki/Abgar_V" title="Abgar V">Abgar V</a> of Edessa (<a href="/wiki/Syriac_Orthodox_Church" title="Syriac Orthodox Church">Syrian Church</a>)</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Alphonsus_Maria_de%27_Liguori" class="mw-redirect" title="Alphonsus Maria de' Liguori">Alphonsus Maria de' Liguori</a></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/%C3%86thelwold_of_Winchester" title="Æthelwold of Winchester">Æthelwold of Winchester</a></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Bernard_V%C3%B5_V%C4%83n_Du%E1%BB%87" class="mw-redirect" title="Bernard Võ Văn Duệ">Bernard Võ Văn Duệ</a> (one of <a href="/wiki/Vietnamese_Martyrs" title="Vietnamese Martyrs">Vietnamese Martyrs</a>)</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Beatification" title="Beatification">Blessed</a> <a href="/wiki/Gerhard_Hirschfelder" title="Gerhard Hirschfelder">Gerhard Hirschfelder</a></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Eusebius_of_Vercelli" title="Eusebius of Vercelli">Eusebius of Vercelli</a></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Exuperius_of_Bayeux" title="Exuperius of Bayeux">Exuperius of Bayeux</a></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Felix_of_Girona" title="Felix of Girona">Felix of Girona</a></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Liberation_of_Saint_Peter" class="mw-redirect" title="Liberation of Saint Peter">Peter Apostle in Chains</a></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Procession_of_the_Cross" class="mw-redirect" title="Procession of the Cross">Procession of the Cross</a> and the beginning of <a href="/wiki/Dormition_of_the_Theotokos#Dormition_fast" class="mw-redirect" title="Dormition of the Theotokos">Dormition Fast</a> (<a href="/wiki/Eastern_Orthodox_Church" title="Eastern Orthodox Church">Eastern Orthodoxy</a>)</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Woman_with_seven_sons" title="Woman with seven sons">The Holy Maccabees</a></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/August_1_(Eastern_Orthodox_liturgics)" title="August 1 (Eastern Orthodox liturgics)">August 1 (Eastern Orthodox liturgics)</a></li></ul></li>
<li>Earliest day on which <a href="/wiki/August_Bank_Holiday_(Ireland)" class="mw-redirect" title="August Bank Holiday (Ireland)">August Bank Holiday (Ireland)</a> can fall, while August 7 is the latest; celebrated on the first Monday of August.</li>
<li>Earliest day on which <a href="/wiki/Civic_Holiday" title="Civic Holiday">Civic Holiday</a> can fall, while August 7 is the latest; celebrated on the first Monday of August. (Canada)</li>
<li>Earliest day on which <a href="/wiki/Commerce_Day" class="mw-redirect" title="Commerce Day">Commerce Day</a>, or <i>Frídagur verslunarmanna</i>, can fall, while August 7 is the latest; celebrated on the first Monday of August. (<a href="/wiki/Iceland" title="Iceland">Iceland</a>)</li>
<li>Earliest day on which <a href="/wiki/Constitution_Day_(Cook_Islands)" class="mw-redirect" title="Constitution Day (Cook Islands)">Constitution Day (Cook Islands)</a> can fall, while August 7 is the latest; celebrated on the first Monday of August.<sup id="cite_ref-41" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-41"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>41<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup></li>
<li>Earliest day on which <a href="/wiki/Farmers%27_Day" title="Farmers' Day">Farmers' Day</a> can fall, while August 7 is the latest; celebrated on the first Monday of August. (<a href="/wiki/Zambia" title="Zambia">Zambia</a>)</li>
<li>Earliest day on which <a href="/wiki/International_Beer_Day" title="International Beer Day">International Beer Day</a> can fall, while August 7 is the latest; celebrated on the first Friday of August.</li>
<li>Earliest day on which <a href="/wiki/Friendship_Day" title="Friendship Day">Friendship Day</a> can fall, while August 7 is the latest; celebrated on the first Sunday of August. (<a href="/wiki/United_States" title="United States">United States</a>)</li>
<li>Earliest day on which <a href="/wiki/Kadooment_Day" class="mw-redirect" title="Kadooment Day">Kadooment Day</a> can fall, while August 7 is the latest; celebrated on the first Monday of August (<a href="/wiki/Barbados" title="Barbados">Barbados</a>)</li>
<li>Earliest day on which <a href="/wiki/Labor_Day_(Samoa)" class="mw-redirect" title="Labor Day (Samoa)">Labor Day (Samoa)</a> can fall, while August 7 is the latest; celebrated on the first Monday of August (<a href="/wiki/Samoa" title="Samoa">Samoa</a>)</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Minden_Day" title="Minden Day">Minden Day</a> (<a href="/wiki/United_Kingdom" title="United Kingdom">United Kingdom</a>)</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/National_Day_(Benin)" class="mw-redirect" title="National Day (Benin)">National Day</a>, celebrates the independence of <a href="/wiki/Benin" title="Benin">Benin</a> from France in 1960.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Swiss_National_Day" title="Swiss National Day">National Day</a>, commemorates Switzerland becoming a single unit in 1291.</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Official_Birthday_and_Coronation_Day_of_the_King_of_Tonga" class="mw-redirect" title="Official Birthday and Coronation Day of the King of Tonga">Official Birthday and Coronation Day of the King of Tonga</a> (<a href="/wiki/Tonga" title="Tonga">Tonga</a>)</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Public_holidays_in_the_Democratic_Republic_of_the_Congo" title="Public holidays in the Democratic Republic of the Congo">Parents' Day</a> (<a href="/wiki/Democratic_Republic_of_the_Congo" title="Democratic Republic of the Congo">Democratic Republic of the Congo</a>)</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Statehood_Day_(Colorado)" class="mw-redirect" title="Statehood Day (Colorado)">Statehood Day (Colorado)</a></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Swiss_National_Day" title="Swiss National Day">Swiss National Day</a> (<a href="/wiki/Switzerland" title="Switzerland">Switzerland</a>)</li>
<li>The beginning of <a href="/wiki/Autumn" title="Autumn">autumn</a> observances in the Northern hemisphere and <a href="/wiki/Spring_(season)" title="Spring (season)">spring</a> observances in the Southern hemisphere (<a href="/wiki/Wheel_of_the_Year" title="Wheel of the Year">Neopagan Wheel of the Year</a>):
<ul><li><a href="/wiki/Lughnasadh" title="Lughnasadh">Lughnasadh</a> in the Northern hemisphere, <a href="/wiki/Imbolc" title="Imbolc">Imbolc</a> in the Southern hemisphere; traditionally begins on the eve of August 1. (<a href="/wiki/Gaels" title="Gaels">Gaels</a>, <a href="/wiki/Ireland" title="Ireland">Ireland</a>, <a href="/wiki/Scotland" title="Scotland">Scotland</a>, <a href="/wiki/Neopagans" class="mw-redirect" title="Neopagans">Neopagans</a>)</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Lammas" title="Lammas">Lammas</a> (<a href="/wiki/England" title="England">England</a>, <a href="/wiki/Scotland" title="Scotland">Scotland</a>, <a href="/wiki/Neopagans" class="mw-redirect" title="Neopagans">Neopagans</a>)</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Pachamama_Raymi" title="Pachamama Raymi">Pachamama Raymi</a> (<a href="/wiki/Quechuan" class="mw-redirect" title="Quechuan">Quechuan</a> in <a href="/wiki/Ecuador" title="Ecuador">Ecuador</a> and <a href="/wiki/Peru" title="Peru">Peru</a>)</li></ul></li>
<li>The first day of <a href="/w/index.php?title=Carnaval_del_Pueblo&action=edit&redlink=1" class="new" title="Carnaval del Pueblo (page does not exist)">Carnaval del Pueblo</a> (<a href="/wiki/Burgess_Park" title="Burgess Park">Burgess Park</a>, <a href="/wiki/London" title="London">London</a>, England)</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Victory_Day" title="Victory Day">Victory Day</a> (<a href="/wiki/Cambodia" title="Cambodia">Cambodia</a>, <a href="/wiki/Laos" title="Laos">Laos</a>, <a href="/wiki/Vietnam" title="Vietnam">Vietnam</a>)</li>
<li><a href="/wiki/World_Scout_Scarf_Day" class="mw-redirect" title="World Scout Scarf Day">World Scout Scarf Day</a></li>

Scope rules preview:
 Title: Scope and Counting Rules — Twitter/X Citations on English Wikipedia August Day Pages (Last June 2023 Versions)

Version: Step 0 (Scope and Rules Definition)
Date: 2025-12-21

1) Scope
- Pages: English Wikipedia date-pages for every day of August: "August 1" through "August 31" (31 pages total). Namespace: Main (article) namespace.
- Language/Project: enwiki (English Wikipedia).
- Target revision for each page: The last revision within June 2023 (UTC). Formally, select the most recent revision whose timestamp is between 2023-06-01T00:00:00Z and 2023-06-30T23:59:59Z, inclusive. If multiple, choose the one with the latest timestamp within this window.
- Fallback if no June 2023 revision exists for a page: Select the latest revision strictly before 2023-07-01T00:00:00Z (i.e., <= 2023-06-30T23:59:59Z). If still none (e.g., page created after July 1, 2023), record as "no eligible revision" and assign count = 0, with a flag in metadata.

2) What to Count
- Objective: For each target page revision, count the number of unique references that cite a specific Twitter/X post (a "tweet").
- Unit of counting: Unique reference entries (i.e., distinct <ref>...</ref> items as they would appear in the References list), not the number of times a named reference is reused in-text.
- Definition of a "Twitter/X post (tweet) citation": A reference whose source ultimately points to a specific tweet. This includes any of the following:
  a) URLs to twitter.com or x.com with a tweet permalink pattern, e.g.,
     - https://twitter.com/{user}/status/{numeric_id}
     - https://x.com/{user}/status/{numeric_id}
     - Historic variants: /statuses/{numeric_id}
     - Mobile/subdomain variants: https://m.twitter.com/{user}/status/{id}, https://mobile.twitter.com/{user}/status/{id}
  b) References using the {{Cite tweet}} template (or equivalent tweet-specific citation templates) with parameters indicating a tweet (e.g., |user=, |author=, |id=, |url= pointing to a tweet). Count as a tweet citation even if URL is missing, provided the template denotes a specific tweet via parameters (e.g., |id= present).
  c) Archived snapshots of tweet URLs via archival services (e.g., web.archive.org, archive.is, archive.today, archive.ph). If the archived URL’s original target (embedded in the archive path) resolves to a tweet permalink as described in (a), count it.

3) Inclusions and Exclusions
- Include:
  - twitter.com and x.com top-level domains and any subdomains (e.g., m.twitter.com, mobile.twitter.com, www.x.com if applicable), provided the path pattern indicates a tweet permalink as above.
  - Shortened URLs (e.g., t.co, bit.ly, tinyurl.com, is.gd, ow.ly, goo.gl, etc.) that ultimately resolve to a tweet permalink on twitter.com or x.com after HTTP redirection.
  - {{Cite tweet}} and equivalent tweet-citation templates even without explicit URLs, if tweet-identifying parameters are present.
  - Archived links to tweets from recognized archival domains when the underlying original is a tweet permalink.
- Exclude:
  - Links to Twitter/X profiles, timelines, search pages, or site home pages that are not specific tweet permalinks.
  - Links solely to Twitter-hosted media endpoints (e.g., pbs.twimg.com, video.twimg.com) without a tweet permalink.
  - Third-party front-ends or mirrors of Twitter content (e.g., nitter.net) unless the reference also clearly specifies the original tweet permalink on twitter.com or x.com.
  - Non-reference external links (e.g., External links section not within <ref> tags) — only <ref>-based citations are counted.

4) Normalization and URL Resolution
- URL normalization steps (applied for identification and deduplication):
  - Lowercase scheme and host; preserve path/case sensitivity as applicable.
  - Remove tracking/query parameters that do not affect tweet identity (e.g., utm_*). For tweets, the identity is determined by domain + canonical path up to the numeric tweet ID.
  - Standardize common mobile/WWW subdomain variants to canonical host for comparison (e.g., m.twitter.com -> twitter.com; mobile.twitter.com -> twitter.com; www.x.com -> x.com).
  - For archived URLs, extract the original URL segment and apply normalization to the original.
- Redirect/shortener resolution:
  - Follow HTTP redirects for known shorteners (t.co, bit.ly, tinyurl.com, is.gd, ow.ly, goo.gl, etc.) up to a reasonable limit.
  - If resolution fails (timeout, HTTP error), fallback logic:
    * If the reference uses {{Cite tweet}} with tweet-identifying params, count it as a tweet citation.
    * Else if the un-resolved short URL domain is t.co and the surrounding citation text/template explicitly indicates a tweet (e.g., publisher=Twitter, work=Twitter, template=Cite tweet), count it.
    * Otherwise, do not count it as a tweet citation.

5) Deduplication Rules
- Primary: Deduplicate by reference identity. Two references are considered the same tweet citation if, after normalization:
  - They resolve to the same tweet permalink (same domain canonicalization + same numeric tweet ID path), OR
  - They are separate <ref> entries that both use {{Cite tweet}} and indicate the same user and same numeric tweet ID.
- Named references reused in-text: Count once (only the defining <ref name="..."> with content forms the unique reference; any <ref name="..."/> reuses are not additional counts).
- Unnamed <ref> entries that independently point to the same tweet permalink count as one after normalization and deduplication.

6) Parsing Sources
- We will parse both:
  - Wikitext of the selected revision to extract <ref> blocks and templates (e.g., {{Cite tweet}}), and
  - The parsed HTML for cross-verification of rendered citations and to catch edge cases from transclusions.
- Priority is given to wikitext extraction for precise template detection; HTML parsing is used for reconciliation and QC.

==Events==
===Pre-1600===
*[[30 BC]] – [[Augustus|Octavian]] (later known as Augustus) enters [[Alexandria]], [[Egypt]], bringing it under the control of the [[Roman Republic]].
*[[AD 69]] – [[Revolt of the Batavi|Batavian rebellion]]: The [[Batavi (Germanic tribe)|Batavians]] in [[Germania Inferior]] (Netherlands) revolt under the leadership of [[Gaius Julius Civilis]].
* [[527]] – [[Justinian I]] becomes the sole ruler of the [[Byzantine Empire]].
* [[607]] – [[Ono no Imoko]] is dispatched as envoy to the [[Sui dynasty|Sui]] court in China (Traditional [[Japanese calendar|Japanese date]]: July 3, 607).
* [[902]] – [[Taormina]], the last Byzantine stronghold in [[Sicily]], is [[Siege of Taormina (902)|captured]] by the [[Aghlabid]] army, concluding the [[Muslim conquest of Sicily]].<ref>{{Byzance et les Arabes|volume=2.1|pages=145–147}}</ref>
*[[1203]] – [[Isaac II Angelos]], restored [[Byzantine Emperor]], declares his son [[Alexios IV Angelos]] co-emperor after pressure from the forces of the [[Fourth Crusade]].
*[[1291]] – The [[Old Swiss Confederacy]] is formed with the signature of the [[Federal Charter of 1291|Federal Charter]].
*[[1469]] – [[Louis XI of France]] founds the [[chivalric order]] called the [[Order of Saint Michael]] in [[Amboise]].
*[[1498]] – [[Christopher Columbus]] becomes the first European to visit what is now [[Venezuela]].
*[[1571]] – The [[Ottoman–Venetian War (1570–73)#Ottoman conquest of Cyprus|Ottoman conquest of Cyprus]] is concluded, by the surrender of [[Famagusta]].
===1601–1900===
*[[1620]] – [[Speedwell (1577 ship)|''Speedwell'']] leaves [[Delfshaven]] to bring [[Pilgrim Fathers|pilgrims]] to [[Plymouth Colony|America]] by way of England.
*[[1664]] – [[Ottoman Empire|Ottoman forces]] are defeated in the [[Battle of Saint Gotthard (1664)|battle of Saint Gotthard]] by an [[Habsburg monarchy|Austrian]] army led by [[Raimondo Montecuccoli]], resulting in the [[Peace of Vasvár]].
*[[1714]] – George, Elector of [[Hanover]], becomes King [[George I of Great Britain]], marking the beginning of the [[Georgian era]] of British history.
*[[1759]] – [[Seven Years' War]]: The [[Battle of Minden]], an allied Anglo-German army victory over the French. In Britain this was one of a number of events that constituted the [[Annus Mirabilis of 1759]] and is celebrated as [[Minden Day]] by certain British Army regiments.
*[[1774]] – British scientist [[Joseph Priestley]] discovers [[oxygen]] gas, corroborating the prior discovery of this element by German-Swedish chemist [[Carl Wilhelm Scheele]].
*[[1798]] – [[French Revolutionary Wars]]: [[Battle of the Nile|Battle of the Nile (Battle of Aboukir Bay)]]: Battle begins when a British fleet engages the French Revolutionary Navy fleet in an unusual night action.<ref>{{cite web |title=Battle of the Nile {{!}} Egyptian-European history |url=https://www.britannica.com/event/Battle-of-the-Nile |website=Encyclopedia Britannica |access-date=15 December 2020 |language=en}}</ref>
*[[1800]] – The [[Acts of Union 1800]] are passed which merge the [[Kingdom of Great Britain]] and the [[Kingdom of Ireland]] into the [[United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland]].
*[[1801]] – [[First Barbary War]]: The American [[schooner]] {{USS|Enterprise|1799|6}} [[Action of 1 August 1801|captures]] the [[Tripoli, Libya|Tripoli]]tan [[polacca]] ''Tripoli'' in a [[single-ship action]] off the coast of modern-day [[Libya]].
*[[1834]] – [[Abolitionism in the United Kingdom|Slavery is abolished in the British Empire]] as the [[Slavery Abolition Act 1833]] comes into force, although it remains legal in the possessions of the [[East India Company]] until the passage of the [[Indian Slavery Act, 1843]].
*  1834   – Construction begins on the [[Wilberforce Monument]] in [[Kingston Upon Hull]].<ref name=Wilberforce>{{Cite web|last=Kitching|first=Sophie|date=9 February 2018|title=The bizarre reason this Hull landmark was moved 82 years ago|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/news/history/bizarre-reason-hull-landmark-moved-1176719|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210302144359/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/news/history/bizarre-reason-hull-landmark-moved-1176719|archive-date=2 March 2021|access-date=1 October 2021|website=[[Hull Daily Mail]]|publisher=[[Trinity Mirror]]|language=en}}</ref>
*[[1842]] – The [[Lombard Street riot]] erupts in [[Philadelphia]], [[Pennsylvania]], United States.
*[[1849]] – ''[[Joven Daniel]]'' wrecks at the coast of [[Araucanía (historic region)|Araucanía]], Chile, leading to allegations that local [[Mapuche]] tribes murdered survivors and kidnapped [[Elisa Bravo]].<ref name=Munoz2010>{{Cite journal|title=El naufragio del bergantín Joven Daniel, 1849. El indígena en el imaginario histórico de Chile|journal=Tiempo Histórico|last=Muñoz Sougarret|first=Jorge|issue=1|year=2010|language=es|pages=133–148}}</ref>
*[[1855]] – The first ascent of [[Monte Rosa]], the second highest summit in the Alps.
*[[1863]] – At the suggestion of Senator [[Johan Vilhelm Snellman|J. V. Snellman]] and the order of Emperor [[Alexander II of Russia|Alexander II]], full rights were promised to the [[Finnish language]] by a language regulation in the [[Grand Duchy of Finland]].<ref>{{cite book| author= Olavi Junnila | title= Suomen historia 5 | year= 1986 | chapter= Autonomian rakentaminen ja kansallisen nousun aika | page= 151 | location = Helsinki | publisher= Weilin + Göös | isbn= 951-35-2494-9 | language = fi}}</ref>
*[[1876]] – [[Colorado]] is admitted as the 38th [[U.S. state]].
*[[1893]] – [[Henry Perky]] patents [[shredded wheat]].
*[[1894]] – The [[Empire of Japan]] and [[Qing dynasty|Qing China]] declare war on each other after a week of fighting over Korea, formally inaugurating the [[First Sino-Japanese War]].
===1901–present===
*[[1907]] – The start of the [[Brownsea Island Scout camp|first Scout camp]] on [[Brownsea Island]], the origin of the worldwide [[Scouting]] movement.
*[[1911]] – [[Harriet Quimby]] takes her pilot's test and becomes the first U.S. woman to earn an Aero Club of America aviator's certificate.
*[[1914]] – The [[German Empire]] declares war on the [[Russian Empire]] at the opening of [[World War I]]. The [[Military of Switzerland|Swiss Army]] mobilizes because of World War I.
*[[1927]] – The [[Nanchang Uprising]] marks the first significant battle in the [[Chinese Civil War]] between the [[Kuomintang]] and [[Chinese Communist Party]]. This day is commemorated as the anniversary of the founding of the [[People's Liberation Army]].
*[[1933]] – Anti-Fascist activists [[Bruno Tesch (antifascist)|Bruno Tesch]], Walter Möller, Karl Wolff and August Lütgens are executed by the Nazi regime in [[Altona, Hamburg|Altona]].
*[[1936]] – The [[1936 Summer Olympics|Olympics]] opened in [[Berlin]] with a ceremony presided over by [[Adolf Hitler]].
*[[1937]] – [[Josip Broz Tito]] reads the resolution "Manifesto of constitutional congress of KPH" to the constitutive congress of KPH ([[League of Communists of Croatia|Croatian Communist Party]]) in woods near [[Samobor]].
*[[1943]] – [[World War II]]: [[Operation Tidal Wave]] also known as "Black Sunday", was a failed American attempt to destroy Romanian oil fields.
*[[1944]] – World War II: The [[Warsaw Uprising]] against the [[Nazism|Nazi German]] occupation breaks out in [[Warsaw]], Poland.
*[[1946]] – Leaders of the [[Russian Liberation Army]], a force of Russian prisoners of war that [[Collaboration with the Axis Powers during World War II|collaborated with Nazi Germany]], are executed in Moscow, [[Soviet Union]] for treason.
*[[1950]] – [[Guam]] is organized as an unincorporated territory of the United States as the President [[Harry S. Truman]] signs the [[Guam Organic Act of 1950|Guam Organic Act]].<ref>{{cite book|author=Department of the Interior|title=Years of Progress, 1945–1952|location=Washington, D.C.|publisher=U.S. Department of the Interior|date=1953|oclc=2183193|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=l3CGAAAAMAAJ|pages=103–104}}</ref>
*[[1957]] – The United States and Canada form the [[North American Aerospace Defense Command]] (NORAD).
*[[1960]] – [[Dahomey]] (later renamed [[Benin]]) declares independence from France.
*[[1960]] – [[Islamabad]] is declared the [[federal capital]] of the [[Government of Pakistan]].
*[[1961]] – U.S. Defense Secretary [[Robert McNamara]] orders the creation of the [[Defense Intelligence Agency]] (DIA), the nation's first centralized military espionage organization.
*[[1964]] – The former [[Belgian Congo]] is renamed the [[Democratic Republic of the Congo]].
*[[1965]] – [[Frank Herbert]]'s novel, [[Dune (novel)|''Dune'']] was published for the first time.  It was named as the world's best-selling science fiction novel in 2003.
*[[1966]] – [[Charles Whitman]] kills 16 people at the [[University of Texas at Austin]] before being killed by the police.
*  1966   – Purges of intellectuals and imperialists becomes official China policy at the beginning of the [[Cultural Revolution]].
*[[1968]] – The coronation is held of [[Hassanal Bolkiah]], the 29th [[Sultan of Brunei]].
*[[1971]] – [[The Concert for Bangladesh]], organized by former Beatle George Harrison, is held at Madison Square Garden in New York City.
*[[1974]] – [[Cyprus dispute]]: The [[United Nations Security Council]] authorizes the [[United Nations Peacekeeping Force in Cyprus|UNFICYP]] to create the "[[United Nations Buffer Zone in Cyprus|Green Line]]", dividing [[Cyprus]] into two zones.
*[[1976]] – [[Niki Lauda]] has a severe accident that almost claims his life at the [[1976 German Grand Prix|German Grand Prix]] at [[Nürburgring|Nurburgring]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=1976 German Grand Prix {{!}} Motorsport Database|url=https://www.motorsportmagazine.com/database/races/1976-german-grand-prix/|access-date=2021-05-17|website=Motorsport Database - Motor Sport Magazine|language=en-GB}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last=Saward|first=Joe|date=2020-10-08|title=How the 1976 German Grand Prix Forever Changed the Fate of the Nurburgring Nordschleife|url=https://www.autoweek.com/racing/formula-1/a34313295/1976-german-grand-prix-changed-fate-nurburgring-nordschleife/|access-date=2021-05-17|website=Autoweek|language=en-US}}</ref>
*[[1980]] – [[Vigdís Finnbogadóttir]] is elected [[President of Iceland]] and becomes the world's first democratically elected female head of state.
*  1980   – A [[Buttevant Rail Disaster|train crash]] kills 18 people in [[County Cork]], [[Republic of Ireland|Ireland]].
*[[1981]] – [[MTV]] begins broadcasting in the United States and airs its first video, "[[Video Killed the Radio Star]]" by [[The Buggles]].
*[[1984]] – Commercial [[peat]]-cutters discover the preserved [[bog body]] of a man, called [[Lindow Man]], at [[Lindow Moss]], [[Cheshire]], England.
*[[1988]] – A British soldier was killed in the [[Inglis Barracks bombing]] in London, England.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://cain.ulst.ac.uk/othelem/chron/ch88.htm#Aug|title=CAIN: Chronology of the Conflict 1988|first=Dr Martin|last=Melaugh|website=Cain.ulst.ac.uk|access-date=4 August 2017}}</ref>
*[[1993]] – The [[Great Mississippi and Missouri Rivers Flood of 1993]] comes to a peak.
*[[2004]] – A [[Ycuá Bolaños supermarket fire|supermarket fire]] kills 396 people and injures 500 others in [[Asunción, Paraguay]].
*[[2007]] – The [[I-35W Mississippi River bridge]] spanning the [[Mississippi River]] in [[Minneapolis|Minneapolis, Minnesota]], collapses during the evening [[rush hour]], killing 13 people and injuring 145.
*[[2008]] – The [[Beijing–Tianjin Intercity Railway]] begins operation as the fastest commuter rail system in the world.
*  2008   – Eleven mountaineers from international expeditions died on K2, the second-highest mountain on Earth in [[2008 K2 disaster|the worst single accident in the history of K2 mountaineering]].
*[[2017]] – A [[suicide attack]] on a mosque in Herat, [[Afghanistan]] kills 20 people.
